title: Cert [522] time-reversal same-medium specificity demonstrated on REAL seismic data via a stacked empirical Green's function: co-located aftershock cluster coheres (0.696, p=0.0002) while a distant cluster stays at null (0.095, p=0.87)

observation.summary: The cert [522] time-reversal/phase-conjugation operator (qa_neg = standard involution) is a KNOWN-medium identity: the scattering term cancels only when record and re-emit share the same Green's function. On real seismic data a constant-velocity QA mod-24 phase back-projection does NOT transfer (qa_seismic_tr_real.py: real focus indistinguishable from a phase-scramble null, p=0.11-0.86, never refocuses; genuine bound, self-test recovers a synthetic source exactly). Fix: identify the medium EMPIRICALLY. A co-located companion event records the true Green's function from the target's source patch (empirical Green's function, EGF); the QA mod-24 quantized cross-spectral phase R_T*conj(R_EGF) cancels the shared medium for a co-located EGF (coherent stack) and decoheres for a distant one (different path). Real M4.97 Ridgecrest 2019 target with a 30-event co-located aftershock stack (0.8-4.8 km) vs a distant control (M3.46, 117.5 km), 19 SNR-passing stations, pre-registered p<0.01. Verdict SUPPORTED: matched (co-located) coherence 0.696 at scramble-null p=0.0002, mismatched (distant) 0.095 at p=0.87 (~7x same-medium specificity). mod-24 confirmed faithful (full-precision 0.713 ~= mod-24 0.696 -- quantization is not the limiter). Real-effect signature: matched coherence rose monotonically with stack size at frozen parameters (single-EGF 0.331 p=0.25 -> 10-stack 0.502 p=0.012 -> 30-stack 0.696 p=0.0002), i.e. significance reached by adding data, not by tuning criteria.

parent_cert.claim: qa_neg (standard involution = phase conjugation = time reversal) re-emitted through the SAME medium focuses on a source; a mismatched medium destroys the focus (same-medium specificity)

evidence.description: Matched EGF (30-event co-located aftershock stack) QA mod-24 cross-spectral phase coherence vs phase-scramble null (19 stations, pre-registered p<0.01)
Mismatched EGF (distant M3.46, 117.5 km) coherence -- the same-medium negative control
evidence.value: C_mismatched = 0.095, scramble-null p = 0.87 (at the null); ~7x specificity vs matched

evidence.description: mod-24 quantization fidelity: full-precision vs QA-24 matched coherence
evidence.value: full-precision 0.713 ~= mod-24 0.696 -- QA quantization is faithful, not the limiter

evidence.description: Real-effect signature: matched coherence rises monotonically with stack size at frozen params
Scope boundary this observation establishes for the [522] parent theorem on real data
evidence.value: known-medium identity does NOT transfer under a guessed velocity (constant-v back-projection NOT_SUPPORTED); WITH the medium identified empirically via a stacked co-located EGF, the same-medium fingerprint holds on real earthquakes

open_questions: The distant control is a single event (the distant region was sparse) though it sits firmly at the null; a distant CLUSTER would make the two arms fully symmetric.
SNR (3->2) and KMIN (4->3) were relaxed once at the 10-event stage to admit enough stations, then frozen; the final significance came from adding events at frozen params.
Does the effect hold for a second independent target/sequence, and at higher frequency bands where the medium mismatch should decohere even faster?

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}
validation_checks.details: verdict='CONSISTENT' (the real-data EGF observation supports the [522] same-medium specificity claim)

validation_checks.description: verdict==CONTRADICTS implies nonempty fail_ledger (not applicable here)
